mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid one-month jump in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals an abrupt labor market deterioration that precedes corporate earnings downgrades, triggering a negative equity return signal over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns following UNRATE jumps > 0.5pp are not statistically lower than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05), or forward 20d returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high inflation readings and rising policy rates create stagflationary expectations that compress both earnings multiples and profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a positive Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or forward 60d returns in high-inflation/high-rate periods are not significantly negative relative to low-inflation/low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index indicates easing supply chain bottlenecks and improved capacity utilization, which supports corporate operating margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum consistently coincides with negative or flat 5-day SPX returns, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% in a 1-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, generating positive forward equity returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns following above-trend GDP prints are not significantly positive, or the rolling 1-year Rank IC of GDPC1 growth against SPX returns remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: The consumer price index crossing the 240 level triggers market repricing of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which elevates term premiums and discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20d returns remain positive or neutral in > 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 240, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth combined with a stable unemployment rate indicates robust labor demand without wage-push inflation overheating, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or forward 20d returns in strong-stable labor periods are not significantly higher than weak-unstable periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts in a low-interest-rate environment signal resilient consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which positively correlates with cyclical equity performance and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Positive HOUST momentum consistently coincides with negative 5-day SPX returns, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS movements in a multivariate regression.
